farmerSupportCenters.purpose: Agronomists based at each center distribute improved, disease-resistant seedlings, run soil analysis, and coach farmers on C.A.F.E. Practices compliance — for free, regardless of whether the farm sells to Starbucks.

roastingPlants.notes: A $220M, 700,000 sq ft integrated roasting & distribution center that cut lead times for roasted coffee reaching Chinese stores from 4–6 weeks (previously imported) to 3–5 days.

combinedRoastingCapacity: Over 1 billion lbs of coffee per year across all plants combined
distribution: Roasted coffee moves from these six plants through a regional distribution-center network to company-operated and licensed stores; packaged/retail products (bagged coffee, K-Cups, RTD bottles) are produced and distributed through Starbucks' consumer packaged goods partnership with Nestlé (global marketing/distribution rights acquired 2018 for $7.15B) and, in North America, a ready-to-drink bottling partnership with PepsiCo dating to 1994.

breakdown.segment: U.S. corporate support, roasting, manufacturing, warehousing & distribution

workforce.termUsed: "Partners" — Starbucks' term for every employee, coined when the company introduced equity (Bean Stock) for all workers, including part-time baristas, in 1991.

benefits.eligibility: Most benefits are available to partners working as few as 20 hours/week
benefits.healthcare: Medical, dental & vision coverage for partners averaging 20+ hours/week
benefits.equity: Bean Stock — annual grant of Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) to eligible partners
benefits.education: Starbucks College Achievement Plan with Arizona State University — 100% tuition coverage for an online bachelor's degree; 35,000+ partners had graduated debt-free by early 2026. From 2026, the plan moves to 100% upfront tuition and extends eligibility to partners' military family members.
benefits.averageTotalCompensation: ~$30/hour in total pay and benefits value for U.S. hourly partners
benefits.fy2025Investment: $500 million allocated to additional store labor hours in the U.S., aimed at cutting partner turnover and rebuilding customer connection
benefits.parentalLeave: Doubled under the 2025 'Back to Starbucks' plan

laborRelations.strike2025: 92% of unionized workers authorized an open-ended strike in early November 2025; a nationwide unfair-labor-practice strike began November 13, 2025 (Red Cup Day) and grew to roughly 2,000+ workers across 65 U.S. cities, lasting 131 days before bargaining resumed in April 2026.
laborRelations.keyDisputes: Wage floor (union proposals ranged from $17–$20/hour starting pay plus ~4% annual raises), minimum staffing levels, and 600+ unresolved unfair-labor-practice charges filed against the company for alleged 'regressive bargaining.'
laborRelations.status: Contract negotiations remain ongoing as of mid-2026; no first contract has yet been ratified.
